title: I Am Still Their Mother
subtitle: An affirmation for mothers who have lost a child
excerpt: An affirmation for mothers in child loss. The motherhood does not end with the death. This practice holds that fact without softening it.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the identity of mother has been brought into question by the loss, or when the world does not seem to know how to hold the motherhood of a child who has died.

content.text: An affirmation for mothers in child loss. The social world often does not know what to do with this kind of motherhood: the motherhood that continues after the child has died. This practice holds the identity directly, without qualification.

The death of a child does not end the motherhood. The love that was built, the pregnancy that happened, the time that was shared: these do not become untrue because the child is not living. The motherhood is permanent.

Say it out loud, with your hand on your heart. Say it as many times as you need to. On days when the world seems to have forgotten, or on days when you almost believe the forgetting yourself: say it again.
